subject: CHEM
title: General Chemistry Laboratory
note: Lab sections meet starting WEEK 1. Students who miss the first 30 minutes of the first lab meeting of the quarter will be administratively dropped from the course. Students are required to pass the Lab Safety Exam for their course in the current term BY MONDAY OF WEEK 1 AT 9AM. Students who do not pass the Lab Safety Requirement will be administratively dropped from the course. Please check the Canvas Safety Site for each lab class you are enrolled in for specific quarter deadlines. Deadline to ADD or DROP without a 'W' for CHEM LAB courses is by the end of the scheduled second lab meeting. For Summer Session, if you drop after the second lab meeting, you will receive a 'W' and will not be eligible for a tuition refund. Policies and Deadlines: https://go.ucsd.edu/4dacX2K, Lab Safety Requirements: https://go.ucsd.edu/43MIfZ9 Students who enroll in CHEM 7L will be charged a $40.00 lab fee that will be assessed with registration fees.

subject: CHEM
title: Special Topics in Biochemistry
note: CHEM 219A Integrated reading and presentation of papers by researchers presenting weekly seminar series sponsored by Biochemistry Division. Attendance at seminar will be part of course. Students should expect to read approximately 3 papers each week, and present once during quarter.

subject: CHEM
title: Seminar/Inorganic Chemistry
note: For Chemistry/Biochemistry graduate students only. Seminars presented by faculty and students on topics of current interest in inorganic chemistry, including areas such as bioinorganic, organometallic and physical-inorganic chemistry. The course is designed to promote a critical evaluation of the available data in specialized areas of inorganic chemistry. Each quarter, three or four different topics will be discussed. Please refer to the department website for specific seminar details: https://chemistry.ucsd.edu/ext/seminars.html.

subject: CHEM
title: Organic Chemistry Seminar
note: Formal seminars or informal puzzle sessions on topics of current interest in organic chemistry, as presented by visiting lectures, local researchers, or students. Please refer to the department website for seminar details: https://chemistry.ucsd.edu/ext/seminars.html. Prerequisites: advanced graduate-student standing. (S/U grades only.) (F, W, S)
